title: securecontext – Development Guide
text: R package memory, knowledge persistence, RAG retrieval, context window management R LLM agents.

title: securecontext – Development Guide
text: S3 classes value objects: document, retriever, context_builder, embedder R6 classes stateful stores: vector_store, knowledge_store TF-IDF embeddings work locally external API Token counting uses word-based approximation (1.3 tokens/word)

title: securecontext – Development Guide
text: test-document.R – S3 document class test-chunk.R – chunking strategies test-embeddings.R – TF-IDF embedder test-vector-store.R – vector store CRUD + search test-knowledge-store.R – JSONL persistence test-retriever.R – end--end retrieval test-context-builder.R – token-aware assembly test-token-count.R – token counting test-integration.R – orchestr/ellmer helpers

title: Getting Started with securecontext
code: library(securecontext)  doc <- document(   "R is a language for statistical computing. It has many packages for data analysis. The tidyverse is a popular collection of packages.",   metadata = list(source = "intro") )  # Different chunking strategies chunks_sent <- chunk_text(doc$text, strategy = "sentence") chunks_para <- chunk_text(doc$text, strategy = "paragraph") chunks_rec  <- chunk_text(doc$text, strategy = "recursive", max_size = 50)

title: Getting Started with securecontext
code: corpus <- c(   "R is great for statistics and data analysis.",   "Python excels at machine learning and deep learning.",   "Julia is fast for numerical computing." )  embedder <- embed_tfidf(corpus) vs <- vector_store$new(dims = embedder$dims)  # Add documents docs <- lapply(corpus, document) ret <- retriever(vs, embedder) add_documents(ret, docs)  # Search results <- retrieve(ret, "statistical computing", k = 2) print(results)

title: Getting Started with securecontext
code: ks <- knowledge_store$new(path = tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl"))  ks$set("user.name", "Alice") ks$set("user.preferences", list(theme = "dark", lang = "R"))  ks$get("user.name") ks$search("^user")

title: Getting Started with securecontext
code: cb <- context_builder(max_tokens = 500) cb <- cb_add(cb, "System instructions go here.", priority = 10, label = "system") cb <- cb_add(cb, "Retrieved context from documents.", priority = 5, label = "context") cb <- cb_add(cb, "Chat history summary.", priority = 3, label = "history")  result <- cb_build(cb) cat(result$context) result$included result$excluded

title: Getting Started with securecontext
text: Use context_for_chat() combine retrieval context building: Wrap knowledge store orchestr:
code: result <- context_for_chat(ret, "statistics", max_tokens = 2000) cat(result$context) mem <- as_orchestr_memory(ks) mem$set("key", "value") mem$get("key")

what: Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents
title: Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents
text: [!CAUTION] Alpha software. package part broader effort Ian Flores Siaca develop proper AI infrastructure R ecosystem. active development used production official release published. APIs may change without notice. Memory, knowledge persistence, RAG retrieval, context management R LLM agents.

title: Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents
Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents
text: Document chunking – fixed-size, sentence, paragraph, recursive strategies TF-IDF embeddings – local embeddings external API required Vector store – -memory cosine similarity search RDS persistence Knowledge store – persistent JSONL key-value storage Semantic retrieval – query documents meaning Context builder – token-aware priority-based context assembly Integration helpers – works orchestr ellmer

title: Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents
code: library(securecontext)  # Create documents docs <- list(   document("R is great for statistics.", metadata = list(topic = "R")),   document("Python excels at machine learning.", metadata = list(topic = "Python")) )  # Build embeddings and index documents emb <- embed_tfidf(vapply(docs, `[[`, character(1), "text")) vs <- vector_store$new(dims = emb$dims) ret <- retriever(vs, emb) add_documents(ret, docs)  # Retrieve relevant context result <- context_for_chat(ret, "statistical computing", max_tokens = 2000) cat(result$context)

Build the context string — cb_build
text: Assembles context including highest-priority items first token limit reached.

title: Build the context string — cb_build
text: list elements context (assembled string), included (labels included items), excluded (labels excluded items), total_tokens (token count assembled context).

title: Fixed-size text chunking — chunk_fixed
text: Splits text chunks approximately size characters optional overlap.

title: Fixed-size text chunking — chunk_fixed
text: text Character string chunk. size Target chunk size characters. overlap Number overlap characters consecutive chunks.

title: Recursive text chunking — chunk_recursive
text: Recursively splits text using hierarchy separators, similar LangChain's recursive text splitter.

title: Recursive text chunking — chunk_recursive
text: text Character string chunk. max_size Maximum chunk size characters. separators Character vector separators try order.

title: Sentence-based text chunking — chunk_sentence
text: Splits text sentence boundaries (period followed space newline).

title: Create a context builder — context_builder
text: max_tokens Maximum number tokens assembled context.

title: Create a context builder — context_builder
code: cb <- context_builder(max_tokens = 100) cb <- cb_add(cb, "Important info", priority = 10) cb <- cb_add(cb, "Less important", priority = 1) result <- cb_build(cb)

Count tokens in text — count_tokens
text: Approximates number tokens using either word-based character-based method.

title: Count tokens in text — count_tokens
text: text Character string (vector) count tokens . method Counting method: "words" (default) multiplies word count 1.3; "chars" divides character count 4.

title: Count tokens in text — count_tokens
code: count_tokens("Hello world, this is a test.") #> [1] 8

title: Create a document — document
text: Constructs S3 object representing text document metadata.

title: Create a document — document
text: text Character string document content. metadata Named list arbitrary metadata. id Optional document identifier. Generated NULL.

title: Create a document — document
code: doc <- document("Hello world", metadata = list(source = "test")) doc #> <securecontext_document> #>   id: doc_1ea7436c156f  #>   chars: 11  #>   metadata: source  #>   text: Hello world

what: securecontext: Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents — securecontext-package
title: securecontext: Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents — securecontext-package
text: Provides memory, knowledge persistence, RAG retrieval, token-aware context window management R LLM agents. Includes document chunking, TF-IDF embeddings, vector search, persistent key-value knowledge stores, priority-based context assembly.

title: securecontext: Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents — securecontext-package
Vector Store — vector_store
text: -memory vector store cosine similarity search RDS persistence.

title: Vector Store — vector_store
text: ids Character vector unique identifiers. embeddings Numeric matrix (nrow = length(ids), ncol = dims). metadata List metadata entries (one per id), empty list.

title: Vector Store — vector_store
text: query_embedding Numeric vector single-row matrix. k Number results return.

title: Vector Store — vector_store
text: Data frame columns id, score, metadata.
